description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) failure mode — InvalidPayload. The discriminator matches a payload variant (Success expects rows: int) but the wire payload is the wrong type ('{"tag":"Success","rows":"lots"}' — rows is a string). parseVariant raises ::ParseError::InvalidPayload(field, reason); the exhaustive !{} handler routes to the InvalidPayload arm and records @parseError = "invalid-payload:" + field, i.e. "invalid-payload:rows". The contract asserts the OFFENDING FIELD NAME (impl-neutral, normative per §41.13) but NOT the `reason` message string (explicit D3 impl-freedom).
rationale: §41.13 (Failure type / variant semantics): InvalidPayload(field, reason) — a variant's payload field has the wrong type or fails a payload predicate during parse. Surfaced at runtime via E-PARSEVARIANT-INVALID-PAYLOAD. The `field` names the offending payload field ('rows') — a compiler-derivable, impl-neutral value; the `reason` string is impl-freedom message text and is deliberately not asserted. The two-field payload destructures per-field in the handler arm (field, reason).
